title: Return and Rest
subtitle: The apostles come back to Jesus from preaching, and the seventy-two are sent and return

stillness: Come apart into a desert place.
Be still here a little while.
Set down, for a moment, the labor of the world.
Contemplation is rightly called a desert: forsaken by many, dwelt in by few.
gospel: And the apostles, gathering together again unto Jesus,
told him all that they had taught in preaching,
and the miracles they had wrought in working.
Come apart into a desert place, and rest a little.
For there were many coming and going,
and they had not so much as time to eat.
After these things the Lord designated also seventy-two others,
and he sent them two and two before his face,
into every city and place whither he himself was to come.
And he said to them: The harvest indeed is great, but the laborers are few.
And the seventy-two returned with joy, saying:
Lord, even the devils are subject to us in thy name.
behold: Behold: the apostles come back to Jesus as to a fountain,
according to that word — Unto the place whence the rivers come, they return, that they may flow again.
They went, like living creatures, from the quiet of contemplation to the labor of action,
and they returned again to the quiet of contemplation.
And he, having compassion on them for their labors, says to them:
Come apart into a desert place, and after labor rest a little.
He led them away, that they might gather themselves in prayer,
as they had been scattered abroad in preaching.
See how small a rest the saints have here, and how great the labor.
And after this the Lord designated the seventy-two,
and sent them two and two before his face —
that they might prepare for him a way and a lodging in the hearts of men.
See them return with joy, glorying in the working of signs:
the devils themselves are subject to us in thy name.
consider: Small is the rest of the saints here, but the labor is great.
Gregory says: so much the greater will that rest be then, as the rest is none now for the love of our Maker.
And understand the reverse, O sluggard: so much the less will that rest be then, as the rest is greater now for the love of the flesh.
Therefore, that you may then rejoice in the sweetness of quiet,
do not now shrink from tasting the bitterness of labor.
The labor of the bees is sweeter, if first a bad taste touch the mouth.
Preachers, after the running about of preaching, ought to return to the secret of contemplation,
and there to weigh their works before God and diligently examine them,
that for the good they may give thanks, and for the failings beg pardon.
No one ought, for the contemplation of God, to set aside the need of his neighbor,
nor, for the need of his neighbor, to despise the contemplation of God.
For what does it profit, if loving ourselves we forsake our neighbors?
Or what does it profit, if loving our neighbors we forsake ourselves?
And because the Lord saw the disciples lifted up over the working of signs,
who from this gloried overmuch — well did Bede say:
They confessed well, giving honor to the name of Christ; but their faith was yet weak: they rejoiced in powers.
prayer: grant me, through the holy preaching of thy disciples,
so to be taught by thy grace,
that I may delight to glory not in vanities,
nor in anything that breeds exaltation and self-importance in me,
but in thy cross and in all things that stir up humility within me.
May that holy Gospel teaching ever confirm and strengthen my heart
in profound humility and in every virtue,
that, cleansed from vices and adorned with virtues,
I may deserve to find rest for my soul, in this present life and in the life to come;
for my heart is restless until it rest in thee, O good Jesus. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Ad locum unde exeunt flumina revertuntur, ut iterum fluant.
latinJewel.english: Unto the place whence the rivers come forth, they return, that they may flow again.
